Definition at line 65 of file build_groups.c.

66{
67
68 /* The algorithm for group construction runs as follows:
69 loop on all collapsed particle
70 + for each particle check its neighbours
71 + if it is a peak of Fmax, make it a one-particle halo
72 + if it touches only one halo:
73 -> check if it should be accreted
74 -> if it should, accrete it
75 -> otherwise, tag it as filament
76 + if it touches more than one halo:
77 -> check if it should be accreted to one halo
78 -> if it should, choose the one it gets nearest to
79 -> then check if all the halo pairs should merge
80 -> if they should, merge them
81 -> if the particle was not accreted before, re-check it
82 -> otherwise, tag it as filament
83 + if it touches only filaments, tag it as filament
84 */
85
86 int merge[NV][NV], neigh[NV], fil_list[NV][4];
87 static int iout, nstep, nstep_p;
88 int nn, ifil, this_z, neigrp, nf, pos;
89 int iz, i1, j1, k1, skip;
90 int ig3, small, large, to_group, accgrp, ig1, ig2;
91 int accrflag, nmerge, peak_cond;
92 double ratio, best_ratio, d2, r2, cputmp;
93 int merge_flag;
94 int ibox, jbox, kbox;
95
96 static int last_z = 0;
97
98 /*
99 counters of various events:
100 0 number of peaks
101 i number of particles with i neighbours (i<=6)
102 7 number of accretion events
103 8 number of accretion events before checking a merger
104 9 number of accretion events after checking a merger
105 10 number of merging events
106 11 number of major mergers
107 12 number of filament particles
108 13 number of accreted filament particles
109 14 number of good halos
110 */
111
112 static unsigned long long counters[NCOUNTERS], all_counters[NCOUNTERS];
113
114#ifdef PLC
115 static int plc_started = 0, last_check_done = 0;
116 int irep, save, mysave, storex, storey, storez;
117 static double NextF_PLC, DeltaF_PLC;
118 double aa, bb, Fplc;
119#endif
120
121 if (first_call)
122 {
123 /* this part contains initializations that should be done at
124 first call */
125 ngroups = FILAMENT; /* group list starts from FILAMENTS */
126 /* filaments are not grouped */
127 for (i1 = 0; i1 <= FILAMENT; i1++)
128 {
129 groups[i1].point = -1;
130 groups[i1].bottom = -1;
131 groups[i1].good = 0;
132 }
133 iout = 0;
134
135 /* sets the counter to zero */
136 for (i1 = 0; i1 < NCOUNTERS; i1++)
137 {
138 counters[i1] = 0;
139 all_counters[i1] = 0;
140 }
141
142 if (!ThisTask)
143 printf("[%s] Starting the fragmentation process to redshift %7.4f\n", fdate(), zstop);
144
145 /* nstep is the number of collapsed particles that will be
146 checked by the code. In classic fragmentation this is the
147 number of particles that collapse by the end of the run. In
148 default fragmentation this selection is performed at
149 distribution time, so this is the number of stored particles.
150 */
151#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
152 nstep = 0;
153 while (frag[indices[nstep]].Fmax >= outputs.Flast)
154 nstep++;
155#else
156 nstep = subbox.Nstored;
157#endif
158 nstep_p = nstep / 20;
159
160#ifdef PLC
161 /* initialization of the root finding routine used by the PLC code */
162 cPLC.function = &condition_F;
163 brent = gsl_root_fsolver_brent;
164 solver = gsl_root_fsolver_alloc(brent);
165 DeltaF_PLC = 0.9; // CAPIRE COME FISSARLO
166 NextF_PLC = plc.Fstart * DeltaF_PLC;
167 brent_err = 1.e-2 * params.InterPartDist;
168 plc.Nstored = plc.Nstored_last = 0;
169#endif
170
171 first_call = 0;
172 }
173 else
174 {
175 if (!ThisTask)
176 printf("[%s] Restarting the fragmentation process to redshift %7.4f\n", fdate(), zstop);
177 }
178
179 /************************************************************************
180 START OF THE CYCLE ON COLLAPSED PARTICLES
181 ************************************************************************/
182 for (this_z = last_z; this_z < nstep; this_z++)
183 {
184 /* In classic fragmentation the particles must be addressed in
185 order of collapse time, while in default fragmentation they
186 are already in that order */
187#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
188 iz = indices[this_z];
189#else
190 iz = this_z;
191#endif
192
193#ifdef PLC
194 /* here it checks if it is time to write the stored PLC halos.
195 If it is the case, it waits for all the tasks to get to the
196 same point and writes the catalog up to this point
197 */
198 if (plc_started && frag[iz].Fmax < NextF_PLC && frag[iz].Fmax >= plc.Fstop)
199 {
200 if (!ThisTask)
201 printf("[%s] Syncing tasks for PLC... Nmax=%d, Nstored=%d, Nstored_last=%d, Fmax=%f\n", fdate(), plc.Nmax, plc.Nstored, plc.Nstored_last, frag[iz].Fmax);
202
203 /* each task checks if the plc buffer is full and it is
204 necessary to write the plc catalog. The criterion is that
205 you need at least space for SAFEPLC times the number of
206 halos that have been updated after last check */
207 mysave = (plc.Nmax - plc.Nstored < SAFEPLC * (plc.Nstored - plc.Nstored_last));
208
209 MPI_Reduce(&mysave, &save, 1, MPI_INT, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
210 MPI_Bcast(&save, 1, MPI_INT, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
211
212 if (!ThisTask)
213 printf("[%s] %d tasks require to store PLC halos at F=%6.3F...\n", fdate(), save, frag[iz].Fmax);
214
215 if (save)
216 {
217 /* Save PLC halos if at least one task asks to */
218 if (write_PLC(0))
219 return 1;
220 plc.Nstored = 0;
221 }
222
223 /* next update will be at this time */
224 NextF_PLC *= DeltaF_PLC;
225 plc.Nstored_last = plc.Nstored;
226 }
227#endif
228
229 /* More initializations */
230
231 neigrp = 0; /* number of neighbouring groups */
232 nf = 0; /* number of neighbouring filament points */
233 accrflag = 0; /* if =1 all the neighbouring filaments are accreted */
234 for (i1 = 0; i1 < NV; i1++)
235 neigh[i1] = 0; /* number of neighbours */
236
237 /*******************************************/
238 /* PARTICLE COORDINATES AND PEAK CONDITION */
239 /*******************************************/
240#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
241 INDEX_TO_COORD(iz, ibox, jbox, kbox, subbox.Lgwbl);
242#else
243 INDEX_TO_COORD(frag_pos[iz], ibox, jbox, kbox, subbox.Lgwbl);
244#endif
245
246 /* skips the peak condition if the point is at the border (and PBCs are not active) */
247 skip = 0;
248 if (!subbox.pbc[_x_] && (ibox == 0 || ibox == subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - 1))
249 ++skip;
250 if (!subbox.pbc[_y_] && (jbox == 0 || jbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - 1))
251 ++skip;
252 if (!subbox.pbc[_z_] && (kbox == 0 || kbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - 1))
253 ++skip;
254
255 particle_name =
256 COORD_TO_INDEX((long long)((ibox + subbox.stabl[_x_] + MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_]) % MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_]),
257 (long long)((jbox + subbox.stabl[_y_] + MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_]) % MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_]),
258 (long long)((kbox + subbox.stabl[_z_] + MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_]) % MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_]),
259 MyGrids[0].GSglobal);
260
261 good_particle = (ibox >= subbox.safe[_x_] && ibox < subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - subbox.safe[_x_] &&
262 jbox >= subbox.safe[_y_] && jbox < subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - subbox.safe[_y_] &&
263 kbox >= subbox.safe[_z_] && kbox < subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - subbox.safe[_z_]);
264
265 if (!skip)
266 {
267 peak_cond = 1;
268 /* checks whether the neighbouring particles collapse later */
269
270 for (nn = 0; nn < NV; nn++)
271 {
272 /* coordinates of the neighbouring particle */
273 switch (nn)
274 {
275 case 0:
276 i1 = (subbox.pbc[_x_] && ibox == 0 ? subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - 1 : ibox - 1);
277 j1 = jbox;
278 k1 = kbox;
279 break;
280 case 1:
281 i1 = (subbox.pbc[_x_] && ibox == subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - 1 ? 0 : ibox + 1);
282 j1 = jbox;
283 k1 = kbox;
284 break;
285 case 2:
286 i1 = ibox;
287 j1 = (subbox.pbc[_y_] && jbox == 0 ? subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - 1 : jbox - 1);
288 k1 = kbox;
289 break;
290 case 3:
291 i1 = ibox;
292 j1 = (subbox.pbc[_y_] && jbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - 1 ? 0 : jbox + 1);
293 k1 = kbox;
294 break;
295 case 4:
296 i1 = ibox;
297 j1 = jbox;
298 k1 = (subbox.pbc[_z_] && kbox == 0 ? subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - 1 : kbox - 1);
299 break;
300 case 5:
301 i1 = ibox;
302 j1 = jbox;
303 k1 = (subbox.pbc[_z_] && kbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - 1 ? 0 : kbox + 1);
304 break;
305 }
306
307 /* accessing the neighbouring particle differs in
308 classic and default fragmentation: in classic
309 fragmentation the information on the neighbour is
310 immediately obtained, in standard fragmentation the
311 neighbour must be seeked in the particle list */
312#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
313 pos = COORD_TO_INDEX(i1, j1, k1, subbox.Lgwbl);
314 neigh[nn] = group_ID[pos];
315 peak_cond &= (frag[iz].Fmax > frag[pos].Fmax);
316#else
317 pos = find_location(i1, j1, k1);
318 if (pos >= 0)
319 {
320 neigh[nn] = group_ID[indices[pos]];
321 peak_cond &= (frag[iz].Fmax > frag[indices[pos]].Fmax);
322 }
323 else
324 neigh[nn] = 0;
325#endif
326
327 /* neighbouring filaments are stored separately */
328 if (neigh[nn] == FILAMENT)
329 {
330 neigh[nn] = 0;
331 fil_list[nf][0] = i1;
332 fil_list[nf][1] = j1;
333 fil_list[nf][2] = k1;
334#ifdef CLASSIC_FRAGMENTATION
335 fil_list[nf][3] = pos;
336#else
337 fil_list[nf][3] = indices[pos];
338#endif
339 nf++;
340 }
341 }
342
343 /* Cleans the list of neighbouring groups removing duplicates */
344 clean_list(neigh);
345
346 /* Number of neighbouring groups */
347 for (nn = neigrp = 0; nn < NV; nn++)
348 if (neigh[nn] > FILAMENT)
349 neigrp++;
350
351 if (neigrp > 0 && good_particle)
352 counters[neigrp]++;
353
354#ifdef PLC
355 /* Past light cone on-the-fly reconstruction: */
356
357 /* is it time to reconstruct the PLC? */
358 if (frag[iz].Fmax < plc.Fstart && frag[iz].Fmax >= plc.Fstop)
359 {
360
361 /* check if this is the first call */
362 if (!plc_started)
363 {
364 plc_started = 1;
365 if (!ThisTask)
366 printf("[%s] Starting PLC reconstruction, Task 0 will store at most %d halos\n", fdate(), plc.Nmax);
367 cputmp = MPI_Wtime();
368 }
369
370 /* PBCs are switched off for this check */
371 storex = subbox.pbc[_x_];
372 storey = subbox.pbc[_y_];
373 storez = subbox.pbc[_z_];
374 subbox.pbc[_x_] = subbox.pbc[_y_] = subbox.pbc[_z_] = 0;
375
376 /* the check is performed on all neighbouring groups */
377 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
378 {
379 /* is the group good and massive enough? */
380 if (neigh[ig1] > FILAMENT &&
381 groups[neigh[ig1]].good &&
382 groups[neigh[ig1]].Mass >= params.MinHaloMass)
383 {
384 thisgroup = neigh[ig1];
385
386 /* loop on replications */
387 /* this loop may be threaded or ported to GPUs,
388 though it's relatively fast */
389 for (irep = 0; irep < plc.Nreplications; irep++)
390 /* checks that the redshift falls in the
391 range of the replication */
392 if (!(frag[iz].Fmax > plc.repls[irep].F1 ||
393 groups[thisgroup].Flast < plc.repls[irep].F2))
394 {
395 replicate[0] = plc.repls[irep].i;
396 replicate[1] = plc.repls[irep].j;
397 replicate[2] = plc.repls[irep].k;
398 /* this computes the difference between
399 the group distance from the observer
400 and its comoving distance at that z */
401 bb = condition_PLC(frag[iz].Fmax);
402
403 /* in this unlikely case we catch the
404 group just on the PLC */
405 if (bb == 0.0)
406 {
407 if (store_PLC(frag[iz].Fmax))
408 return 1;
409 }
410 else if (bb > 0.)
411 {
412 /* if it is outside the PLC then check
413 whether it has just passed since
414 last check */
415 aa = condition_PLC(groups[thisgroup].Flast);
416 if (aa < 0.0)
417 {
418 /* in this case the group has
419 passed through the PLC since
420 last check, then compute its
421 time of PLC cross */
422 if ((Fplc = find_brent(groups[thisgroup].Flast, frag[iz].Fmax)) == -99.00)
423 return 1;
424 /* and store it */
425 if (store_PLC(Fplc))
426 return 1;
427 }
428 }
429 }
430 }
431 /* updates the Flast field */
432 groups[neigh[ig1]].Flast = frag[iz].Fmax;
433 }
434 /* restoring PBCs */
435 subbox.pbc[_x_] = storex;
436 subbox.pbc[_y_] = storey;
437 subbox.pbc[_z_] = storez;
438 }
439 else if (plc.Fstart > 0. && frag[iz].Fmax < plc.Fstart)
440 {
441 /* if nothing must be done, only update the Flast field */
442 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
443 groups[neigh[ig1]].Flast = frag[iz].Fmax;
444 }
445
446#endif
447 }
448 else
449 {
450 /* this closes the if (!skip) condition above: if the
451 particle is at the border of the domain it will not be a
452 peak and will have no neighbours */
453 peak_cond = 0;
454 neigrp = 0;
455 }
456
457 /* Is the point a peak? */
458 if (peak_cond)
459 {
460 /**********************************************************************
461 FIRST CASE: PEAK
462 **********************************************************************/
463
464 /* New group */
465 if (good_particle)
466 counters[0]++;
467
468 ngroups++;
469
470 /* this paranoid check will raise an error in case of major bugs */
471 if (ngroups > Npeaks + 2)
472 {
473 printf("OH MY DEAR, TASK %d FOUND TOO MANY GROUPS AT z=%f, STEP %d OF %d, THIS SHOULD NOT HAPPEN!\n", ThisTask, frag[iz].Fmax - 1.0, this_z, nstep);
474 return 1;
475 }
476
477 /* sets the properties of a one-particle group */
478 groups[ngroups].t_peak = frag[iz].Fmax;
479 groups[ngroups].t_appear = -1;
480 groups[ngroups].t_merge = -1;
481 groups[ngroups].Pos[0] = ibox + SHIFT;
482 groups[ngroups].Pos[1] = jbox + SHIFT;
483 groups[ngroups].Pos[2] = kbox + SHIFT;
484 groups[ngroups].Vel[0] = frag[iz].Vel[0];
485 groups[ngroups].Vel[1] = frag[iz].Vel[1];
486 groups[ngroups].Vel[2] = frag[iz].Vel[2];
487#ifdef TWO_LPT
488 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT[0] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT[0];
489 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT[1] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT[1];
490 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT[2] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT[2];
491#ifdef THREE_LPT
492 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1[0] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1[0];
493 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1[1] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1[1];
494 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1[2] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1[2];
495 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2[0] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2[0];
496 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2[1] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2[1];
497 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2[2] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2[2];
498#endif
499#endif
500#ifdef RECOMPUTE_DISPLACEMENTS
501 groups[ngroups].Vel_prev[0] = frag[iz].Vel_prev[0];
502 groups[ngroups].Vel_prev[1] = frag[iz].Vel_prev[1];
503 groups[ngroups].Vel_prev[2] = frag[iz].Vel_prev[2];
504#ifdef TWO_LPT
505 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT_prev[0] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT_prev[0];
506 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT_prev[1] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT_prev[1];
507 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT_prev[2] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT_prev[2];
508#ifdef THREE_LPT
509 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[0] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[0];
510 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[1] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[1];
511 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[2] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1_prev[2];
512 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[0] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[0];
513 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[1] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[1];
514 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[2] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2_prev[2];
515#endif
516#endif
517#endif
518 groups[ngroups].Mass = 1;
519 groups[ngroups].name = particle_name;
520 groups[ngroups].good = good_particle;
521 groups[ngroups].point = iz;
522 groups[ngroups].bottom = iz;
523 groups[ngroups].ll = ngroups;
524 groups[ngroups].halo_app = ngroups;
525#ifdef PLC
526 if (frag[iz].Fmax > plc.Fstart)
527 groups[ngroups].Flast = plc.Fstart;
528 else
529 groups[ngroups].Flast = frag[iz].Fmax;
530#endif
531
532 group_ID[iz] = ngroups;
533 linking_list[iz] = iz;
534 if (params.MinHaloMass == 1)
535 {
536 groups[ngroups].t_appear = frag[iz].Fmax;
537#ifdef SNAPSHOT
538 frag[iz].zacc = frag[iz].Fmax - 1;
539#endif
540 }
541 }
542 else if (neigrp == 1)
543 {
544
545 /**********************************************************************
546 SECOND CASE: 1 GROUP
547 **********************************************************************/
548
549 /* if the points touches only one group, check whether to accrete the point on it */
550
551 condition_for_accretion(1, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[0], &d2, &r2);
552
553 if (d2 < r2)
554 {
555
556 /*********************
557 accretion on a group!
558 *********************/
559
560 if (good_particle)
561 counters[7]++;
562 accrflag = 1;
563 to_group = neigh[0];
564 accretion(to_group, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax);
565 }
566 else
567 {
568 /*********
569 filament!
570 *********/
571
572 if (good_particle)
573 counters[12]++;
574 groups[FILAMENT].Mass++;
575 group_ID[iz] = FILAMENT;
576 linking_list[iz] = iz;
577 }
578 }
579 else if (neigrp > 1)
580 {
581 /**********************************************************************
582 THIRD CASE: >1 GROUP
583 **********************************************************************/
584
585 /* In this case the point touches more than one group */
586
587 /*********************
588 accretion on a group?
589 *********************/
590
591 best_ratio = pow(10. * subbox.Lgwbl[_x_], 2.0);
592 accgrp = -1;
593 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
594 {
595 condition_for_accretion(2, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[ig1], &d2, &r2);
596 ratio = d2 / r2;
597 if (ratio < 1.0 && ratio < best_ratio)
598 {
599 best_ratio = ratio;
600 accgrp = ig1;
601 }
602 }
603
604 if (accgrp >= 0)
605 {
606 if (good_particle)
607 {
608 counters[7]++;
609 counters[8]++;
610 }
611 accrflag = 1;
612 to_group = neigh[accgrp];
613 accretion(neigh[accgrp], ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax);
614 }
615
616 /* Then checks by pairs whether the groups must be merged together */
617
618 nmerge = 0;
619 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
620 for (ig2 = 0; ig2 < ig1; ig2++)
621 {
622 merge[ig1][ig2] = 0;
623 condition_for_merging(frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[ig1], neigh[ig2], &merge_flag);
624 if (merge_flag)
625 {
626 merge[ig1][ig2] = 1;
627 nmerge++;
628 }
629 }
630
631 /******************
632 merging of groups!
633 ******************/
634
635 /* The group number of the largest group is preserved */
636
637 if (nmerge > 0)
638 {
639 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
640 for (ig2 = 0; ig2 < ig1; ig2++)
641 if (merge[ig1][ig2] == 1 && neigh[ig1] != neigh[ig2])
642 {
643 if (good_particle)
644 counters[10]++;
645 if (groups[neigh[ig1]].Mass > groups[neigh[ig2]].Mass)
646 {
647 merge_groups(neigh[ig1], neigh[ig2], frag[iz].Fmax);
648 large = neigh[ig1];
649 small = neigh[ig2];
650 }
651 else
652 {
653 merge_groups(neigh[ig2], neigh[ig1], frag[iz].Fmax);
654 small = neigh[ig1];
655 large = neigh[ig2];
656 }
657 if (to_group == small)
658 to_group = large;
659 for (ig3 = 0; ig3 < neigrp; ig3++)
660 if (neigh[ig3] == small)
661 neigh[ig3] = large;
662
663 if (groups[large].Mass < 5 * groups[small].Mass && good_particle)
664 counters[11]++;
665 }
666 }
667
668 /* If relevant, it tries again to accrete the particle */
669
670 if (accgrp == -1)
671 {
672 clean_list(neigh);
673
674 /* Number of neighbouring groups */
675 for (nn = neigrp = 0; nn < NV; nn++)
676 if (neigh[nn] > FILAMENT)
677 neigrp++;
678
679 best_ratio = pow(10. * subbox.Lgwbl[_x_], 2.0);
680 accgrp = -1;
681 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
682 {
683 condition_for_accretion(3, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[ig1], &d2, &r2);
684 ratio = d2 / r2;
685 if (ratio < best_ratio)
686 {
687 best_ratio = ratio;
688 accgrp = ig1;
689 }
690 }
691
692 if (best_ratio < 1)
693 {
694 if (good_particle)
695 {
696 counters[7]++;
697 counters[9]++;
698 }
699 accrflag = 1;
700 to_group = neigh[accgrp];
701 accretion(neigh[accgrp], ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax);
702 }
703 else
704 {
705 /* If the point has not been accreted at all: */
706
707 /*********
708 filament!
709 *********/
710
711 if (good_particle)
712 counters[12]++;
713 groups[FILAMENT].Mass++;
714 group_ID[iz] = FILAMENT;
715 linking_list[iz] = iz;
716 }
717 }
718 }
719 else
720 {
721 /**********************************************************************
722 FOURTH CASE: FILAMENTS
723 **********************************************************************/
724 if (good_particle)
725 counters[12]++;
726 groups[FILAMENT].Mass++;
727 group_ID[iz] = FILAMENT;
728 linking_list[iz] = iz;
729
730 /****************/
731 /* end of cases */
732 /****************/
733 }
734
735 /* If the particle was accreted somewhere, checks whether to
736 accrete to the same halo all the neighbouring filaments;
737 first it checks conditions for all filament particles, then
738 it accretes those that should */
739
740 if (accrflag && nf && !skip)
741 {
742 /* loop on filament particles */
743 for (ifil = 0; ifil < nf; ifil++)
744 {
745 condition_for_accretion(4, fil_list[ifil][0], fil_list[ifil][1], fil_list[ifil][2],
746 fil_list[ifil][3], frag[iz].Fmax, to_group, &d2, &r2);
747 /* tags filaments that should be accreted (the condition
748 should be checked without changing the halo) */
749 if (d2 < r2)
750 fil_list[ifil][3] *= -1;
751 }
752 /* accrete all filament particles */
753 for (ifil = 0; ifil < nf; ifil++)
754 if (fil_list[ifil][3] < 0)
755 {
756 fil_list[ifil][3] *= -1;
757 accretion(to_group, fil_list[ifil][0], fil_list[ifil][1],
758 fil_list[ifil][2], fil_list[ifil][3], frag[iz].Fmax);
759
760 groups[FILAMENT].Mass--;
761
762 if (fil_list[ifil][0] >= subbox.safe[_x_] &&
763 fil_list[ifil][0] < subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - subbox.safe[_x_] &&
764 fil_list[ifil][1] >= subbox.safe[_y_] &&
765 fil_list[ifil][1] < subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - subbox.safe[_y_] &&
766 fil_list[ifil][2] >= subbox.safe[_z_] &&
767 fil_list[ifil][2] < subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - subbox.safe[_z_])
768 {
769 counters[7]++;
770 counters[13]++;
771 counters[12]--;
772 }
773 }
774 }
775
776#ifdef PLC
777 /* if this is the end of the cycle for PLC, perform a last check
778 on all halos to see if some has passed through the PLC in
779 the meantime */
780 /* Note: this part is largely a replication of the PLC code above */
781 if (plc.Fstart > 0 && !last_check_done &&
782 (this_z == nstep - 1 || frag[iz].Fmax < plc.Fstop))
783 {
784
785 /* first write to disc what you have */
786 if (write_PLC(0))
787 return 1;
788 plc.Nstored = 0;
789
790 /* PBCs are switched off for this check */
791 storex = subbox.pbc[_x_];
792 storey = subbox.pbc[_y_];
793 storez = subbox.pbc[_z_];
794 subbox.pbc[_x_] = subbox.pbc[_y_] = subbox.pbc[_z_] = 0;
795
796 last_check_done = 1;
797 /* the check is performed on ALL groups */
798 for (ig1 = FILAMENT + 1; ig1 <= ngroups; ig1++)
799 {
800 /* is the group alive, good and massive enough? */
801 if (groups[ig1].point >= 0 && groups[ig1].good &&
802 groups[ig1].Mass >= params.MinHaloMass)
803 {
804 thisgroup = ig1;
805
806 /* loop on replications */
807 /* this loop may be threaded or ported to GPUs,
808 though it's relatively fast */
809 for (irep = 0; irep < plc.Nreplications; irep++)
810 /* checks that the redshift falls in the
811 range of the replication */
812 if (groups[ig1].Flast > plc.repls[irep].F2)
813 {
814 replicate[0] = plc.repls[irep].i;
815 replicate[1] = plc.repls[irep].j;
816 replicate[2] = plc.repls[irep].k;
817 /* this computes the difference between the
818 group distance from the observer and its
819 comoving distance at that z */
820 bb = condition_PLC(plc.Fstop);
821
822 /* in this unlikely case we catch the group
823just on the PLC */
824 if (bb == 0.0)
825 {
826 if (store_PLC(plc.Fstop))
827 return 1;
828 }
829 else if (bb > 0.)
830 {
831 /* if it is outside the PLC then check
832 whether it has just passed since
833 last check */
834 aa = condition_PLC(groups[ig1].Flast);
835 if (aa < 0.0)
836 {
837 /* in this case the group has passed
838through the PLC since last time */
839 if ((Fplc = find_brent(groups[ig1].Flast, plc.Fstop)) == -99.0)
840 return 1;
841 if (store_PLC(Fplc))
842 return 1;
843 }
844 }
845 }
846 }
847 }
848
849 /* restoring PBCs */
850 subbox.pbc[_x_] = storex;
851 subbox.pbc[_y_] = storey;
852 subbox.pbc[_z_] = storez;
853
854 if (!ThisTask)
855 printf("[%s] PLC: Last check on groups done, Task 0 stored %d halos (max:%d)\n",
856 fdate(), plc.Nstored, plc.Nmax);
857 cputime.plc += MPI_Wtime() - cputmp;
858
859 if (write_PLC(1))
860 return 1;
861 }
862#endif
863
864 /************************************************************************
865 CLOSING THE CYCLE
866 ************************************************************************/
867
868 /* prints the fraction of steps done */
869 if (!ThisTask && !(this_z % nstep_p))
870 printf("[%s] *** %3d%% done, F = %6.2f, z = %6.2f\n", fdate(),
871 this_z / (nstep_p) * 5, frag[iz].Fmax,
872 frag[iz].Fmax - 1.0);
873
874 /* Write output if relevant.
875 The while cycle is here because it may happen that some
876 outputs are requested when there are very few collapsed
877 particles, and if no particle collapses between two catalogs
878 one catalog may not be written; better to have an empty
879 catalog, or two identical ones */
880 while (this_z == nstep - 1 || frag[iz].Fmax < outputs.F[iout])
881 {
882 cputmp = MPI_Wtime();
883
884 if (!ThisTask)
885 printf("[%s] Writing output at z=%f\n", fdate(),
886 outputs.z[iout]);
887
888 fflush(stdout);
889 MPI_Barrier(MPI_COMM_WORLD);
890
891 /* halo catalog */
892 if (write_catalog(iout))
893 return 1;
894
895 /* halo mass function */
896 if (compute_mf(iout))
897 return 1;
898
899 /* merger histories, only at the last redshift */
900 if (iout == outputs.n - 1)
901 {
902 if (write_histories())
903 return 1;
904 }
905
906 cputime.io += MPI_Wtime() - cputmp;
907
908 /* if we are at the end, just break out of the while cycle */
909 iout++;
910 if (this_z == nstep - 1)
911 break;
912 }
913
914 if (this_z != nstep - 1 && frag[iz].Fmax < zstop + 1.0)
915 {
916 if (!ThisTask)
917 printf("[%s] Pausing fragmentation process\n", fdate());
918 last_z = this_z + 1;
919 return 0;
920 }
921
922 /************************************************************************
923 END OF DO-CYCLE ON COLLAPSED POINTS
924 ************************************************************************/
925 }
926
927 /* Counters */
928 for (ig1 = FILAMENT + 1; ig1 <= ngroups; ig1++)
929 if (groups[ig1].point >= 0 && groups[ig1].good)
930 counters[14]++;
931
932 MPI_Reduce(counters, all_counters, NCOUNTERS, MPI_UNSIGNED_LONG_LONG, MPI_SUM, 0, MPI_COMM_WORLD);
933
934 /* statistics */
935 if (!ThisTask)
936 {
937 printf("Total number of peaks: %Lu\n", all_counters[0]);
938 printf("Total number of good halos: %Lu\n", all_counters[14]);
939 printf("Particles with N neighbouring groups: %Lu %Lu %Lu %Lu %Lu %Lu\n",
940 all_counters[1], all_counters[2], all_counters[3],
941 all_counters[4], all_counters[5], all_counters[6]);
942 printf("Total number of accretion events: %Lu\n", all_counters[7]);
943 printf("Accretion before evaluating merger: %Lu\n", all_counters[8]);
944 printf("Accretion after evaluating merger: %Lu\n", all_counters[9]);
945 printf("Accretion of filament particles: %Lu\n", all_counters[13]);
946 printf("\n");
947 printf("Global stats at the final redshift:\n");
948 printf("Total number of merger events: %Lu\n", all_counters[10]);
949 printf("Total number of major merger events: %Lu\n", all_counters[11]);
950 printf("Final number of filament particles: %Lu\n", all_counters[12]);
951 printf("Final number of particles in halos: %Lu\n", all_counters[0] + all_counters[7]);
952 printf("Total number of collapsed particles: %Lu\n", all_counters[0] + all_counters[7] + all_counters[12]);
953 printf("Total number of uncollapsed particles: %Lu\n", MyGrids[0].Ntotal - all_counters[0] - all_counters[7] - all_counters[12]);
954 printf("\n");
955 }
956
957#ifdef SNAPSHOT
958 /* Saving group_ID in the frag structure for the snapshot*/
959 groups[0].name = 0;
960 groups[FILAMENT].name = FILAMENT;
961 for (iz = 0; iz < subbox.Nstored; iz++)
962 frag[iz].group_ID = groups[group_ID[iz]].name;
963#endif
964
965#ifdef PLC
966
967 /* close the root solver */
968 gsl_root_fsolver_free(solver);
969
970#endif
971
972 /* Bye! */
973
974 return 0;
975}

References _x_, _y_, _z_, accretion(), group_data::bottom, brent, brent_err, clean_list(), compute_mf(), condition_F(), condition_for_accretion(), condition_for_merging(), condition_PLC(), COORD_TO_INDEX, cPLC, cputime, output_data::F, replication_data::F1, replication_data::F2, fdate(), FILAMENT, find_brent(), find_location(), output_data::Flast, group_data::Flast, product_data::Fmax, frag, frag_pos, plc_data::Fstart, plc_data::Fstop, group_data::good, good_particle, group_ID, groups, grid_data::GSglobal, group_data::halo_app, replication_data::i, INDEX_TO_COORD, indices, param_data::InterPartDist, cputime_data::io, replication_data::j, replication_data::k, subbox_data::Lgwbl, linking_list, group_data::ll, group_data::Mass, merge_groups(), param_data::MinHaloMass, MyGrids, output_data::n, group_data::name, NCOUNTERS, ngroups, plc_data::Nmax, plc_data::Nreplications, subbox_data::Nstored, plc_data::Nstored, plc_data::Nstored_last, NV, outputs, params, particle_name, subbox_data::pbc, cputime_data::plc, plc, group_data::point, group_data::Pos, replicate, plc_data::repls, subbox_data::safe, SAFEPLC, SHIFT, solver, subbox_data::stabl, store_PLC(), subbox, group_data::t_appear, group_data::t_merge, group_data::t_peak, thisgroup, ThisTask, product_data::Vel, group_data::Vel, product_data::Vel_2LPT, group_data::Vel_2LPT, product_data::Vel_2LPT_prev, group_data::Vel_2LPT_prev, product_data::Vel_3LPT_1, group_data::Vel_3LPT_1, product_data::Vel_3LPT_1_prev, group_data::Vel_3LPT_1_prev, product_data::Vel_3LPT_2, group_data::Vel_3LPT_2, product_data::Vel_3LPT_2_prev, group_data::Vel_3LPT_2_prev, product_data::Vel_prev, group_data::Vel_prev, write_catalog(), write_histories(), write_PLC(), output_data::z, and product_data::zacc.

◆ clean_list()

void clean_list ( int *  arr)

978{

1700 /* Condition for a group being inside or outside the PLC
1701 It computes the difference between the comoving distance of the
1702 group from the observer (given the replication) and the comoving
1703 distance at redshift z=F-1 */
1704
1705 int i;
1706 double diff1, condition;

1714 condition += diff1 * diff1;
1715 }
1716
1717 condition = sqrt(condition) - ComovingDistance((double)F - 1.0) / params.InterPartDist;

1521{
1522 /* it moves an object from the Lagrangian to the Eulerian space,

1524
1525 PRODFLOAT pos;

1839{
1840 /* limited and quick version of build_groups:
1841 no PLC, no counters, no outputs */
1842 int merge[NV][NV], neigh[NV], fil_list[NV][4];
1843 int nn, ifil, neigrp, pos;
1844 int iz, i1, j1, k1, skip;
1845 int ig3, small, large, nf, to_group, accgrp, ig1, ig2;
1846 int accrflag, nstep, nmerge, nstep_p, peak_cond;
1847 double ratio, best_ratio, d2, r2;
1848 int merge_flag;
1849 int ibox, jbox, kbox;
1850
1851 /* Initializations */
1852
1853 ngroups = FILAMENT; // number of groups + filaments
1854 groups[FILAMENT].point = groups[FILAMENT].bottom = subbox.Nstored; // filaments are not grouped!
1855 for (i1 = 0; i1 < FILAMENT; i1++) // this is probably unnecessary, but better add it
1856 {
1857 groups[i1].point = -1;
1858 groups[i1].good = 0;
1859 }
1860
1861 if (!ThisTask)
1862 printf("[%s] Starting the quick fragmentation process\n", fdate());
1863
1864 /* Calculates the number of steps required */
1865
1866 nstep = subbox.Nstored;
1867 nstep_p = nstep / 5;
1868
1869 /************************************************************************
1870 START OF THE CYCLE ON POINTS
1871 ************************************************************************/
1872 for (iz = 0; iz < nstep; iz++)
1873 {
1874 /* More initializations */
1875
1876 neigrp = 0; // number of neighbouring groups
1877 nf = 0; // number of neighbouring filament points
1878 accrflag = 0; // if =1 all the neighbouring filaments are accreted
1879 for (i1 = 0; i1 < NV; i1++)
1880 neigh[i1] = 0; // number of neighbours
1881
1882 /* grid coordinates from the indices (sub-box coordinates) */
1883 INDEX_TO_COORD(frag_pos[iz], ibox, jbox, kbox, subbox.Lgwbl);
1884
1885 /* skips if the point is at the border (and PBCs are not active) */
1886 skip = 0;
1887 if (!subbox.pbc[_x_] && (ibox == 0 || ibox == subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - 1))
1888 ++skip;
1889 if (!subbox.pbc[_y_] && (jbox == 0 || jbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - 1))
1890 ++skip;
1891 if (!subbox.pbc[_z_] && (kbox == 0 || kbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - 1))
1892 ++skip;
1893
1894 particle_name =
1895 COORD_TO_INDEX((long long)((ibox + subbox.stabl[_x_] + MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_]) % MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_x_]),
1896 (long long)((jbox + subbox.stabl[_y_] + MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_]) % MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_y_]),
1897 (long long)((kbox + subbox.stabl[_z_] + MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_]) % MyGrids[0].GSglobal[_z_]),
1898 MyGrids[0].GSglobal);
1899
1900 good_particle = (ibox >= subbox.safe[_x_] && ibox < subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - subbox.safe[_x_] &&
1901 jbox >= subbox.safe[_y_] && jbox < subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - subbox.safe[_y_] &&
1902 kbox >= subbox.safe[_z_] && kbox < subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - subbox.safe[_z_]);
1903
1904 if (!skip)
1905 {
1906 peak_cond = 1;
1907 /* checks whether the neighbouring particles collapse later */
1908 for (nn = 0; nn < NV; nn++)
1909 {
1910 switch (nn)
1911 {
1912 case 0:
1913 i1 = (subbox.pbc[_x_] && ibox == 0 ? subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - 1 : ibox - 1);
1914 j1 = jbox;
1915 k1 = kbox;
1916 break;
1917 case 1:
1918 i1 = (subbox.pbc[_x_] && ibox == subbox.Lgwbl[_x_] - 1 ? 0 : ibox + 1);
1919 j1 = jbox;
1920 k1 = kbox;
1921 break;
1922 case 2:
1923 i1 = ibox;
1924 j1 = (subbox.pbc[_y_] && jbox == 0 ? subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - 1 : jbox - 1);
1925 k1 = kbox;
1926 break;
1927 case 3:
1928 i1 = ibox;
1929 j1 = (subbox.pbc[_y_] && jbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_y_] - 1 ? 0 : jbox + 1);
1930 k1 = kbox;
1931 break;
1932 case 4:
1933 i1 = ibox;
1934 j1 = jbox;
1935 k1 = (subbox.pbc[_z_] && kbox == 0 ? subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - 1 : kbox - 1);
1936 break;
1937 case 5:
1938 i1 = ibox;
1939 j1 = jbox;
1940 k1 = (subbox.pbc[_z_] && kbox == subbox.Lgwbl[_z_] - 1 ? 0 : kbox + 1);
1941 break;
1942 }
1943
1944 pos = find_location(i1, j1, k1);
1945 if (pos >= 0)
1946 {
1947 neigh[nn] = group_ID[indices[pos]];
1948 peak_cond &= (frag[iz].Fmax > frag[indices[pos]].Fmax);
1949 }
1950 else
1951 neigh[nn] = 0;
1952
1953 if (neigh[nn] == FILAMENT)
1954 {
1955 neigh[nn] = 0;
1956 fil_list[nf][0] = i1;
1957 fil_list[nf][1] = j1;
1958 fil_list[nf][2] = k1;
1959 fil_list[nf][3] = indices[pos];
1960 nf++;
1961 }
1962 }
1963
1964 /* Cleans the list of neighbouring groups */
1965 clean_list(neigh);
1966
1967 /* Number of neighbouring groups */
1968 for (nn = neigrp = 0; nn < NV; nn++)
1969 if (neigh[nn] > FILAMENT)
1970 neigrp++;
1971 }
1972 else
1973 {
1974 peak_cond = 0;
1975 neigrp = 0;
1976 }
1977
1978 /* Is the point a peak? */
1979 if (peak_cond)
1980 {
1981 /**********************************************************************
1982 FIRST CASE: PEAK
1983 **********************************************************************/
1984 /* New group */
1985 ngroups++;
1986 groups[ngroups].t_peak = frag[iz].Fmax;
1987 groups[ngroups].t_appear = -1;
1988 groups[ngroups].t_merge = -1;
1989 groups[ngroups].Pos[0] = ibox + SHIFT;
1990 groups[ngroups].Pos[1] = jbox + SHIFT;
1991 groups[ngroups].Pos[2] = kbox + SHIFT;
1992 groups[ngroups].Vel[0] = frag[iz].Vel[0];
1993 groups[ngroups].Vel[1] = frag[iz].Vel[1];
1994 groups[ngroups].Vel[2] = frag[iz].Vel[2];
1995#ifdef TWO_LPT
1996 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT[0] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT[0];
1997 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT[1] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT[1];
1998 groups[ngroups].Vel_2LPT[2] = frag[iz].Vel_2LPT[2];
1999#ifdef THREE_LPT
2000 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1[0] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1[0];
2001 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1[1] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1[1];
2002 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_1[2] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_1[2];
2003 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2[0] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2[0];
2004 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2[1] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2[1];
2005 groups[ngroups].Vel_3LPT_2[2] = frag[iz].Vel_3LPT_2[2];
2006#endif
2007#endif
2008 groups[ngroups].Mass = 1;
2009 groups[ngroups].name = particle_name;
2010 groups[ngroups].good = good_particle;
2011 groups[ngroups].point = iz;
2012 groups[ngroups].bottom = iz;
2013 groups[ngroups].ll = ngroups;
2014 groups[ngroups].halo_app = ngroups;
2015 group_ID[iz] = ngroups;
2016 linking_list[iz] = iz;
2017 }
2018 else if (neigrp == 1)
2019 {
2020
2021 /**********************************************************************
2022 SECOND CASE: 1 GROUP
2023 **********************************************************************/
2024 /* if the points touches only one group, check whether to accrete the point on it */
2025
2026 condition_for_accretion(1, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[0], &d2, &r2);
2027 if (d2 < r2)
2028 {
2029 accrflag = 1;
2030 to_group = neigh[0];
2031 accretion(to_group, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax);
2032 }
2033 else
2034 {
2035 groups[FILAMENT].Mass++;
2036 group_ID[iz] = FILAMENT;
2037 linking_list[iz] = iz;
2038 }
2039 }
2040 else if (neigrp > 1)
2041 {
2042 /**********************************************************************
2043 THIRD CASE: >1 GROUP
2044 **********************************************************************/
2045 /* In this case the point touches more than one group */
2046
2047 best_ratio = pow(10. * subbox.Lgwbl[_x_], 2.0);
2048 accgrp = -1;
2049 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
2050 {
2051 condition_for_accretion(2, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[ig1], &d2, &r2);
2052 ratio = d2 / r2;
2053 if (ratio < 1.0 && ratio < best_ratio)
2054 {
2055 best_ratio = ratio;
2056 accgrp = ig1;
2057 }
2058 }
2059 if (accgrp >= 0)
2060 {
2061 accrflag = 1;
2062 to_group = neigh[accgrp];
2063 accretion(neigh[accgrp], ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax);
2064 }
2065 /* Then checks whether the groups must be merged together */
2066 nmerge = 0;
2067 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
2068 for (ig2 = 0; ig2 < ig1; ig2++)
2069 {
2070 merge[ig1][ig2] = 0;
2071 condition_for_merging(frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[ig1], neigh[ig2], &merge_flag);
2072 if (merge_flag)
2073 {
2074 merge[ig1][ig2] = 1;
2075 nmerge++;
2076 }
2077 }
2078
2079 /******************
2080 merging of groups!
2081 ******************/
2082
2083 /* The group number of the largest group is preserved */
2084
2085 if (nmerge > 0)
2086 {
2087 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
2088 for (ig2 = 0; ig2 < ig1; ig2++)
2089 if (merge[ig1][ig2] == 1 && neigh[ig1] != neigh[ig2])
2090 {
2091 if (groups[neigh[ig1]].Mass > groups[neigh[ig2]].Mass)
2092 {
2093 merge_groups(neigh[ig1], neigh[ig2], frag[iz].Fmax);
2094 large = neigh[ig1];
2095 small = neigh[ig2];
2096 }
2097 else
2098 {
2099 merge_groups(neigh[ig2], neigh[ig1], frag[iz].Fmax);
2100 small = neigh[ig1];
2101 large = neigh[ig2];
2102 }
2103 if (to_group == small)
2104 to_group = large;
2105 for (ig3 = 0; ig3 < neigrp; ig3++)
2106 if (neigh[ig3] == small)
2107 neigh[ig3] = large;
2108 }
2109 }
2110
2111 /* If relevant, it tries again to accrete the particle */
2112
2113 if (accgrp == -1)
2114 {
2115 clean_list(neigh);
2116
2117 /* Number of neighbouring groups */
2118 for (nn = neigrp = 0; nn < NV; nn++)
2119 if (neigh[nn] > FILAMENT)
2120 neigrp++;
2121
2122 best_ratio = pow(10. * subbox.Lgwbl[_x_], 2.0);
2123 accgrp = -1;
2124 for (ig1 = 0; ig1 < neigrp; ig1++)
2125 {
2126 condition_for_accretion(3, ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax, neigh[ig1], &d2, &r2);
2127 ratio = d2 / r2;
2128 if (ratio < best_ratio)
2129 {
2130 best_ratio = ratio;
2131 accgrp = ig1;
2132 }
2133 }
2134
2135 if (best_ratio < 1)
2136 {
2137 accrflag = 1;
2138 to_group = neigh[accgrp];
2139 accretion(neigh[accgrp], ibox, jbox, kbox, iz, frag[iz].Fmax);
2140 }
2141 else
2142 {
2143 /* If the point has not been accreted at all: */
2144 groups[FILAMENT].Mass++;
2145 group_ID[iz] = FILAMENT;
2146 linking_list[iz] = iz;
2147 }
2148 }
2149 }
2150 else
2151 {
2152 /**********************************************************************
2153 FOURTH CASE: FILAMENTS
2154 **********************************************************************/
2155 groups[FILAMENT].Mass++;
2156 group_ID[iz] = FILAMENT;
2157 linking_list[iz] = iz;
2158
2159 /* end of cases */
2160 }
2161
2162 /* Checks whether to accrete all the neighbouring filaments;
2163 first it checks conditions for all filament particles,
2164 then it accretes those that should */
2165 if (accrflag && nf && !skip)
2166 {
2167 for (ifil = 0; ifil < nf; ifil++)
2168 {
2169 condition_for_accretion(4, fil_list[ifil][0], fil_list[ifil][1], fil_list[ifil][2],
2170 fil_list[ifil][3], frag[iz].Fmax, to_group, &d2, &r2);
2171 if (d2 < r2)
2172 fil_list[ifil][3] *= -1;
2173 }
2174
2175 for (ifil = 0; ifil < nf; ifil++)
2176 if (fil_list[ifil][3] < 0)
2177 {
2178 fil_list[ifil][3] *= -1;
2179 accretion(to_group, fil_list[ifil][0], fil_list[ifil][1],
2180 fil_list[ifil][2], fil_list[ifil][3], frag[iz].Fmax);
2181 groups[FILAMENT].Mass--;
2182 }
2183 }
2184
2185 /************************************************************************
2186 END OF DO-CYCLE ON COLLAPSED POINTS
2187 ************************************************************************/
2188
2189 /* Fraction of steps done */
2190 if (!ThisTask && !(iz % nstep_p))
2191 printf("[%s] *** %3d%% done, F = %6.2f, z = %6.2f\n", fdate(),
2192 iz / (nstep_p) * 20, frag[iz].Fmax,
2193 frag[iz].Fmax - 1.0);
2194 }
2195
2196 return 0;
2197}

1385{
1386 /* sets the weight in a pos_data object for interpolating displacements */
1387 if (!ScaleDep.myseg)
1388 {
1389 /* at the first fragmentation segment, the interpolation weight
1390 is just the growing mode at redshift F-1 divided by the "final" one */
1391 myobj->w = GrowingMode(myobj->z, myobj->myk) / GrowingMode(ScaleDep.z[ScaleDep.myseg], myobj->myk);
1392#ifdef TWO_LPT
1393 myobj->w2 = GrowingMode_2LPT(myobj->z, myobj->myk) / GrowingMode_2LPT(ScaleDep.z[ScaleDep.myseg], myobj->myk);

1396 myobj->w32 = GrowingMode_3LPT_2(myobj->z, myobj->myk) / GrowingMode_3LPT_2(ScaleDep.z[ScaleDep.myseg], myobj->myk);
1397#endif
1398#endif
1399 }
1400 else
1401 {
1402 /* after, the weight linearly interpolates between the two redshifts */
1403 myobj->w = (GrowingMode(myobj->z, myobj->myk) - GrowingMode(ScaleDep.z[ScaleDep.myseg - 1], myobj->myk)) /
1404 (GrowingMode(ScaleDep.z[ScaleDep.myseg], myobj->myk) - GrowingMode(ScaleDep.z[ScaleDep.myseg - 1], myobj->myk));

1740 return 0;
1741 }
1742
1743 set_obj(thisgroup, F, &obj1);
1744 set_obj_vel(thisgroup, F, &obj1);
1745 for (i = 0; i < 3; i++)
1746 {
1747 /* displacement is done up to ORDER_FOR_CATALOG */
1748 x[i] = params.InterPartDist *
1749 (q2x(i, &obj1, subbox.pbc[i], (double)subbox.Lgwbl[i], ORDER_FOR_CATALOG) + subbox.stabl[i] - (plc.center[i] - MyGrids[0].GSglobal[i] * replicate[i]));
1750 }
1751
1752 coord_transformation_cartesian_polar(x, &rhor, &theta, &phi);
1753 if (90. - theta < params.PLCAperture)
1754 {
1755
1756 plcgroups[plc.Nstored].z = F - 1.0;
1757 plcgroups[plc.Nstored].Mass = groups[thisgroup].Mass;
1758 plcgroups[plc.Nstored].name = groups[thisgroup].name;
1759
1760 for (i = 0; i < 3; i++)
1761 {
1762 /* displacement is done up to ORDER_FOR_CATALOG */
1763 plcgroups[plc.Nstored].x[i] = x[i];
1764 plcgroups[plc.Nstored].v[i] = vel(i, &obj1);
1765 }
1766 /* plcgroups[plc.Nstored].rhor=rhor; */
1767 /* plcgroups[plc.Nstored].theta=theta; */
1768 /* plcgroups[plc.Nstored].phi=phi; */
1769
1770 int iz = (int)((plcgroups[plc.Nstored].z - params.LastzForPLC) / plc.delta_z);
1771 if (iz == plc.nzbins)
1772 iz--;
1773 plc.nz[iz] += 1.0;
1774
1775 plc.Nstored++;
1776 }
1777
1778 return 0;
1779}

1641 {

1169{
1170 /* Upgrade the history of a group: g1 flows into g2 */
1171
1172 int old_i;
1173
1174 /* if groups have no branches */
1175 if (groups[g1].ll == g1 && groups[g2].ll == g2)
1176 {
1177 groups[g1].ll = g2;
1178 groups[g2].ll = g1;
1179 }
1180 /* if g1 has branches but g2 is a single halo */
1181 else if (groups[g1].ll != g1 && groups[g2].ll == g2)
1182 {
1183 groups[g2].ll = g1;
1184 old_i = g1;
1185 while (groups[old_i].ll != g1)
1186 old_i = groups[old_i].ll;
1187 groups[old_i].ll = g2;
1188 }
1189 /* if g1 is a single halo and g2 has branches */
1190 else if (groups[g1].ll == g1 && groups[g2].ll != g2)
1191 {
1192 old_i = g2;
1193 while (groups[old_i].ll != g2)
1194 {
1195 old_i = groups[old_i].ll;
1196 groups[old_i].halo_app = g1;
1197 }
1198 groups[g2].halo_app = g1;
1199 groups[g1].ll = groups[g2].ll;
1200 groups[g2].ll = g1;
1201 }
1202 else
1203 /* both the groups have branches */
1204 {
1205 old_i = g2;
1206 while (groups[old_i].ll != g2)
1207 {
1208 old_i = groups[old_i].ll;
1209 groups[old_i].halo_app = g1;
1210 }

1212 while (groups[old_i].ll != g1)

1214 groups[old_i].ll = groups[g2].ll;
1215 groups[g2].ll = g1;

1217

PRODFLOAT virial ( int  mass,
PRODFLOAT  F,

)

1011{
1012 /* This function is at the root of the decision of whether particles
1013 should be accreted and halos should be merged. For a group of a
1014 given mass (in number of particles) it returns the SQUARE of its
1015 "virial radius", that is the capture radius for accretion and
1016 merging events.
1017 flag=1 for accretion, flag=1 for merging.
1018 */
1019
1020 PRODFLOAT r2, rlag, sigmaD;
1021
1022 /* the Lagrangian radius in grid units is just the cubic root of the
1023 halo mass */
1024 rlag = pow((double)mass, 0.333333333333333);
1025 int S = Smoothing.Nsmooth - 1;
1026 /* this is the linear density rms on the grid */
1027 sigmaD = sqrt(Smoothing.TrueVariance[S]) *
1028#ifdef SCALE_DEPENDENT
1029 GrowingMode((double)F - 1., Smoothing.k_GM_dens[S]); // ATTENZIONE, QUESTO NON E` DEL TUTTO CORRETTO IN MG
1030#else
1031 GrowingMode((double)F - 1., params.k_for_GM);
1032#endif
1033 if (!flag)
1034 /* merging */
1035 r2 = pow(f_m * pow(rlag, espo) * (sigmaD > sigmaD0 ? 1.0 + (sigmaD - sigmaD0) * f_rm : 1.0), 2.0) + pow(f_200 * rlag, 2.0);
1036 else
1037 /* accretion */
1038 r2 = pow(f_a * pow(rlag, espo) * (sigmaD > sigmaD0 ? 1.0 + (sigmaD - sigmaD0) * f_ra : 1.0), 2.0) + pow(f_200 * rlag, 2.0);
1039
1040 /*-----------------MODIFIED GRAVITY WITH f(R)-----------------*/
1041
1042 // DEVO CAPIRE COS'E` QUESTO CODICE QUI SOTTO!!!
1043
1044#ifdef SALTALA
1045 // FILE *output_file_rlag = fopen("output_rlag_values.txt", "w");
1046 int interpolation_Done = 0;
1047
1048 for (int iradius = 0; iradius <= S; iradius++)
1049 {
1050 if (rlag * params.InterPartDist >= Smoothing.Radius[iradius])
1051 {
1052
1053 // Check if interpolation has already been done for this rlag
1054 if (interpolation_Done == 0)
1055 {
1056
1057 // Perform reverse linear interpolation to find the k value
1058 // printf("your rlag is smaller than this smoothing radius: %.6f\n", Smoothing.Radius[iradius - 1]);
1059 double r_lower = Smoothing.Radius[iradius];
1060 double r_upper = Smoothing.Radius[iradius - 1];
1061 double k_lower = Smoothing.k_GM_dens[iradius];
1062 double k_upper = Smoothing.k_GM_dens[iradius - 1];
1063
1064 // Reverse linear interpolation formula
1065 // Risultato identico all'interpolazione lineare di GSL. Si potrebbe usare per l'interpolazione nei tempi di collasso? Così si può portare su GPU senza GSL !
1066 // double k_interpolated = k_lower + (k_upper - k_lower) * ((rlag * params.InterPartDist - r_lower) / (r_upper - r_lower));
1067
1068 // Define arrays for r and k values
1069 double r_values[2] = {r_lower, r_upper};
1070 double k_values[2] = {k_lower, k_upper};
1071
1072 // Perform linear interpolation using GSL
1073 gsl_interp *interp = gsl_interp_alloc(gsl_interp_linear, 2);
1074 gsl_interp_init(interp, r_values, k_values, 2);
1075
1076 double r_value = rlag * params.InterPartDist;
1077 double k_interpolated = gsl_interp_eval(interp, r_values, k_values, r_value, NULL);
1078
1079 // Print data to the terminal for debugging in an organized layout
1080 // printf("rlag: %.6f\t r_lower: %.6f\t r_upper: %.6f\t k_lower: %.6f\t k_upper: %.6f\t k_interpolated: %.6f\n",
1081 // rlag * params.InterPartDist, r_lower, r_upper, k_lower, k_upper, k_interpolated);
1082 // printf("\n");
1083 // Set the flag to indicate that interpolation has been done for this rlag
1084 interpolation_Done = 1;
1085 // gsl_interp_free(interp);
1086 }
1087 }
1088 }
1089
1090 // fclose(output_file_rlag);
1091
1092#endif
1093
1094 return r2;
1095}
